title: Erigeron elongatiformis
intro: Erigeron elongatiformis, also known as Mountain Fleabane, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that may aid in wound healing and the management of respiratory issues such as bronchitis. The bioactive constituents of Erigeron elongatiformis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Erigeron elongatiformis is classified within the plant kingdom as a member of the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Erigeron, which consists of approximately 60 species of flowering plants. The genus Erigeron is further classified within the tribe Astereae, which contains over 800 species of daisy-like plants. Erigeron elongatiformis is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and is often found in dry, rocky areas. Its classification is a part of the broader classification system of flowering plants, which is used to understand the evolutionary relationships between different species.

In traditional medicine, it has been used to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and flowers are thought to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
